newsSince: Hua Hong posted a Q1 2026 beat with raised guidance (stock jumped ~12% on the print), a faster-than-expected Fab9A ramp, Fab9B construction starting in 2026, and the Huali Micro (HLMC) acquisition accepted by the SSE and expected to close in H2 2026. Q1 capex was ~$924.9M for 12-inch expansion; Q2 guided to $690-700M revenue with 14-16% GM, supported by AI-driven demand and tight 12-inch supply/pricing power.

sources.label: Hua Hong Q1 beat and raised guidance (Yahoo)
Hua Hong valuation after Q1 growth & capacity plans (Simply Wall St)

verdicts.rankRationale: Fundamentals improved since June 9 (Q1 beat + raised guide, faster Fab9A ramp, Huali Micro deal progressing, 12-inch pricing power), but on the CPO lens Hua Hong's direct co-packaged-optics exposure is low. Slight up only; +105% YTD and mature-node pricing risk cap CPO-relevant alpha.
verdicts.bull: 12-inch capacity ramp, raised guide and tight specialty supply lift pricing; Huali Micro adds scale.
verdicts.neutral: Strong foundry execution but minimal direct CPO/photonics relevance; rerated +105% YTD.
verdicts.bear: China mature-node pricing pressure and capex intensity; CPO exposure is peripheral.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: 12-inch capacity ramp, pricing power and Huali Micro close
verdicts.primaryRisk: China mature-node pricing and capex intensity
